Section 1: Consent & Safety
Please read carefully before continuing
1.1  Do you agree to take part in this survey?
By selecting Yes, you confirm you are 18 or over, your answers are anonymous, you can stop at any time, and you understand this AI tool is not a therapist.

column7: 3.2  Has anything happened recently that had a strong effect on your emotions?

Section 4 — Putting a Name to It
Now let's think about specific emotions

4.1  Which of these emotions best describe how you're feeling right now? (Select all that apply)
column9: 4.2  How strong would you say that emotion (or those emotions) feel right now?

We want to understand how people cope, and what support feels most useful

column11: 5.2  If an AI reflected your feelings back to you after reading what you'd written, what kind of response would feel most helpful?

column12: AI is good to some extent but sometimes the flow and sincerity of the response can be disrupted because you sense the responses are kind of superficial, especially bc of the robotic tone to the AI. I also don’t like the idea of it consistently trying to ‘ground’ your emotion or taking something positive and trying to sort of dim its light by telling you that YES it may have been a good moment but you still need to stay realistic- if you get the gist? It can sort of diminish the excitement of the moment- realistically a friend would hype you up or be excited with you.
